title: Design System: Can I Eat This?

body.purpose: Every piece of text in the interface, one voice throughout.

label.purpose: Captions, prices, helper text, small UI labels.

motion.name: ease-hover
motion.purpose: Border and background transitions on hover for pickable tiles and buttons.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The one clear next action on a screen, e.g. New dish, Ask.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #3b82f6; color: #ffffff; padding: 8px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: none; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2563eb; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #60a5fa; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button / Pickable Tile
components.description: Dish tiles, allergen tiles, and lower-emphasis actions like back or Ask about another allergen.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #111827; color: #f3f4f6; padding: 12px 16px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #1f2937; font-weight: 500; font-size: 14px; text-align: left; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #1f2937; border-color: #3b82f6; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #60a5fa; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Result Card - Safe
components.description: The signature component. Reports a confirmed-safe result.
components.html:
Safe

Jacket potato with beans

No milk listed in the confirmed allergens.
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px 20px; border-width: 2px; border-style: solid; } .ds-card-safe { background: #052e16; border-color: #166534; } .ds-card-heading { font-weight: 700; font-size: 18px; margin: 0 0 4px; color: #86efac; } .ds-card-item { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0; }

components.name: Result Card - Unsafe
components.description: Reports a confirmed-unsafe result. Same weight and structure as Safe, only the color changes.
components.html:
Unsafe

Margherita pizza slice

Margherita pizza slice is confirmed to contain milk.
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px 20px; border-width: 2px; border-style: solid; } .ds-card-unsafe { background: #450a0a; border-color: #991b1b; } .ds-card-heading { font-weight: 700; font-size: 18px; margin: 0 0 4px; color: #f87171; } .ds-card-item { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0; }

components.name: Result Card - Unverified
components.description: Reports an ambiguous or missing-data result. Must carry the exact same visual weight as Safe and Unsafe, never look like an error.
components.html:
Can't confirm - ask staff

Veggie chilli (vegan?)

Menu data is flagged ambiguous (may contain traces of nuts) - cannot confirm safe.
components.css: .ds-card { border-radius: 16px; padding: 24px 20px; border-width: 2px; border-style: solid; } .ds-card-unverified { background: #422006; border-color: #854d0e; } .ds-card-heading { font-weight: 700; font-size: 18px; margin: 0 0 4px; color: #facc15; } .ds-card-item { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0 0 8px; } .ds-card-body { font-size: 14px; color: #d1d5db; margin: 0; }

components.name: Free-text Input
components.description: The fallback question box for anything that doesn't fit the two-tap flow.
components.html: <input class="ds-input" placeholder="e.g. is this okay for a shellfish allergy?" />
components.css: .ds-input { background: #111827; color: #f3f4f6; border: 1px solid #1f2937; border-radius: 8px; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 14px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #9ca3af; } .ds-input:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #3b82f6; }

narrative.overview: Dark, quiet, low-glare, the way a well-run kitchen pass looks at 11pm: nothing shouting for attention except the one thing that needs to be seen right now. Almost the entire surface sits in a narrow band of near-black neutrals with no decoration, no gradients, no shadows, no accent color competing for attention. Color only ever appears to mean something. The signal blue shows up on interactive elements a user needs to find and nowhere else. The safe/unsafe/unverified triad is the one place saturated color is allowed, and it always arrives with a plain-language label next to it, never alone.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black neutral canvas, flat, no shadows
One interactive accent (signal blue), used sparingly
Three semantic status colors that are the actual product, never decorative
Large tap targets, short copy, built to be read in two seconds

rules.name: The One Meaning Rule
rules.body: Saturated color never appears for decoration. If a color isn't the signal accent or a safety status, it doesn't belong on this screen.

rules.name: The Never-Lesser Rule
rules.body: The unverified/amber state is styled with exactly the same visual weight as safe and unsafe. It must never look like a fallback or an error.

rules.name: The Single Voice Rule
rules.body: Do not introduce a second font family for emphasis. Weight and size carry hierarchy, not a display face.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: No box-shadow anywhere in the system. If something needs to feel closer to the user, lighten its background one step and brighten its border.

dos: Do keep color meaning-only: signal blue for interaction, the semantic triad for results, nothing else
Do give the unverified/amber state the same visual weight as safe and unsafe
Do keep every result labeled in text, never rely on color alone
Do keep the whole interface flat
Do size tap targets for a stranger glancing at a phone or counter screen
donts: Don't add a hero-metric card, a gradient accent, an eyebrow label, or a numbered section marker
Don't introduce a second typeface for personality
Don't use playful, game-like, or cutesy visual language
Don't let the amber/unverified card read as broken, muted, or lower-priority
